title: Offside
text: Law 11 - Offside. A player is in an offside position if any part of the head, body or feet is in the opponents' half (excluding the halfway line) and any part of the head, body or feet is nearer to the opponents' goal line than both the ball and the second-last opponent. The hands and arms of all players, including the goalkeepers, are not considered. It is not an offence to be in an offside position; a player in an offside position commits an offence only if, at the moment the ball is played or touched by a team-mate, they become involved in active play by interfering with play, interfering with an opponent, or gaining an advantage. A player in their own half cannot be in an offside position.

title: Fouls and Misconduct
text: Law 12 - Fouls and Misconduct. A direct free kick is awarded if a player commits an offence against an opponent in a manner considered careless, reckless or using excessive force, such as charging, jumping at, kicking, pushing, striking, tackling or holding. Handball offences and certain other offences are also penalised. A penalty kick is awarded if such an offence is committed inside the offender's penalty area.

title: Free Kicks
text: Law 13 - Free Kicks. Free kicks are direct or indirect. The ball must be stationary when kicked and is in play when it is kicked and clearly moves. Opponents must be at least 9.15 metres from the ball until it is in play.

title: The Penalty Kick
text: Law 14 - The Penalty Kick. A penalty kick is awarded if a player commits a direct-free-kick offence inside their own penalty area or off the field as part of play. The ball is placed on the penalty mark and the goalkeeper must remain on the goal line, facing the kicker, with at least part of one foot on or in line with the goal line until the ball is kicked.

title: The Referee
text: Law 5 - The Referee. Each match is controlled by a referee who has full authority to enforce the Laws of the Game. The referee may be assisted by the video assistant referee (VAR), who may help only in the case of a clear and obvious error or serious missed incident relating to a goal/no goal, penalty/no penalty, direct red card, and mistaken identity.
